msContents.summary: A compilation of material relating to the admiral of England and the admiralty court. The contents are very similar to the Black Book of the Admiralty (Kew, National Archives, HCA 12) and a compilation of admiralty material now in the British Library (Cotton MS. Vespasian B. xxii). Printed with discussion Monumenta Juridica, The Black Book of the Admiralty, ed. Travers Twiss, Rolls Series 55, 4 vols. (London, 1871-76); see also 'Admiralty Courts', Medieval England Maritime Project

msItem.incipit: Pvis que aucun est fait admiral, premierement lui fault ordonner & substituer desoubz lui pour estre ses lieutenantz
msItem.note: Twiss, pp. 1-23. A 3-line decorated initial on fol. 2v ('Apres qune nauire', Twiss p. 9 c. 8).

msItem.incipit: Pardeuant nous auons traitte de lesleccion de ladmiral, or faisons ordannance comment ladmiral soy doit gouuerner
msItem.explicit: Et cest le Iugement en cest cas
msItem.note: Twiss pp. 23-44. No heading; introduced by 4-line initial. At fol. 9r-v, introduced by a 2-line flourished initial, is the oath of a juror in the admiralty court, in Middle English:
msItem.incipit: This here see my lord the admirall that I John atte Nasshe schall well & trewly enquere for oure lord the kyng and wel and trewly at this tyme to yow at this court of thadmiralte present as muche as I haue in knowleche
msItem.explicit: I shall so doo the kyngis counseill my felawes and myn own wel & trewly hele withoute fraude or malengyn, so god my help & the holy doom and by this book.

msItem.incipit: Soit enquis des larrons es ports comme de cordes bateaux et autres appareils des nefs
msItem.explicit: pour garder & sauluer les coustumes du roy

msItem.title: Laws of Oleron
msItem.incipit: Premierement len fait ung homme maistre dune nef
msItem.note: Twiss pp. 88-130; the expanded version in 35 chapters which is uniquely transmitted in the present manuscript, the Black Book, and Cotton MS. Vesp. B. xxii: K-F. Krieger, Ursprung und Wurzeln der Rôles de Oléron, Quellen und Darstellungen zur hansischen Geschichte n. F., 15 (Köln, 1970), esp. pp. 12-17; cf. 'The Laws of Oléron', Medieval England Maritime Project.
Twiss, pp. 132-172; on the text cf. R. Ward, Medieval Shipmaster, ch. 1 and appendix 2.
Accounting aids: note of months and days in a year; calculations of annual equivalents of per diem rates; conversion tables; lists of wages. (Twiss, pp. 174-177)

msItem.incipit: Licet circa ciuilia iudicia multa concurrant, hoc tamen vt plurimum contingere solent ... Adibit actor iudicem competentem id est habentem iurisdictiones ordinariam vel delegatam et talem qui iudex esse possit, ut superius dictum est
msItem.author: Rolandus de Passageriis
msItem.title: Summa artis notariae
msItem.note: The rest of fol. 64r is blank.
Twiss, pp. 178-220; for the Summa see ed. Venice, 1498-9, fols. 53r-63r (BSB-Ink R-222)

msItem.rubric: Theoffice of the Marshall in thabsence of thadmirall
msItem.incipit: Furst in thabsence of thadmirall of Englond or his lieuetenaunt the seid marshall shall take and resceyue all pleyntes that longith vnto thoffice of thadmiraltie, and to take plegge to answer to the processe after cours of lawe
msItem.explicit: Item for euery arrest made out of the court at the request of partie he shall haue and take after his labour and travaile that is to sey for euerey myle iiij d cariyng the prisoner with hym
msItem.note: Added in a slightly later hand. For an Anglo-Norman version see Twiss, p. 406 (from Cotton MS. Vespasian B. xxii, fol. 98r).

msItem.rubric: The liberties of ye cinquports. Copia vera.

supportDesc.collation: Separate endleaves, fols. i (a lifted pastedown), ii, iii (fols. ii-iii a bifolium, originally blank, fol. i and stub documentary waste, see below); 1(8)-8(8) (fols. 1-64), 9(2) (fols. 65-6, ruled, originally blank); followed by a quire of 8 leaves, blank and unruled, the last leaf pasted down (fols. 67-73 and pastedown). Fols. 1-64: catchwords, some scribal; leaf signatures in the usual form.

layoutDesc.layout: Ruled in purple ink for 1 col. of 26 lines, with single vertical and double horizontal bounding lines. Ruled space c. 135 c. 82 . Fol. 33r: (34 + [134] + 72) × (35 + [82] + 44)

handDesc.handNote: Formal cursive bookhand (bastard secretary) by one main hand.

decoDesc.summary: Pächt and Alexander iii. 1041, pl. XCVI:
decoDesc.decoNote: 9-line decorated initial mostly in gold, green, blue and rose, fol. 1r, extending into a full border in the same colours; other initials fols. 2v, 5v, 10r, 19r, 28v.
Elsewhere 2- or 1-line blue initials flourished in red at the beginning of each paragraph (omitted in error, fol. 16r, erased fol. 42r)

binding.p: Original inboard binding of alum-tawed skin, stained pink, over wooden boards; sewn on five double alum-tawed thongs; remains on the front board of two strap-and-pin fastenings, closing left to right.

origin.origDate: 15th century, probably end of the third quarter or beginning of the last quarter

history.provenance: The left pastedown is documentary waste from an enrolled account; the surviving entry reads 'Hospicium. Expense hospicij Johanne regine Anglie' 4 April 12 Henry VI - 4 April 13 Henry VI (1434-5). On the right pastedown 'vyelle', 15th-16th century. Fol. ii recto, 15th century, 'Amen est finis salutis'.
'1550. Loyalte na hount. E. Clynton' (fol. iii verso) i. e. Edward lord Clinton, high admiral of England 1550-4 and 1557-85.
William Hareward: '1579. Liber Gulielmi Hareward supremę Curiae Admiralitatis Angliae Registrarii' (fol. ii recto), who added the text on fol. 65r and probably the marginal rubrics.
The letters 'C H' are on fols. 14, 14 and elsewhere, no doubt (as Selden suggests) the initials of Charles Howard lord Howard of Effingham, earl of Nottingham, high admiral 1585-1619.

John Selden, 1584–1654. Perhaps by 1635, when cited in Selden's Mare Clausum (p. 265); described in Selden's Vindiciae ... de scriptione Maris Clausi (1653), p. 10 as having been in Selden's possession 'per annos complures'.

recordHist.source: Description by Matthew Holford, March 2026. Previously described: Summary Catalogue. Summary Catalogue, Vol. 2 Part 1, p. 610 Summary Catalogue, Vol. 2 Part 1, p. 611

revisionDesc.change: Add binding information from Summary Catalogue.

Toby Burrows/Mapping Manuscript MigrationsProvenance and acquisition information added using https://github.com/mapping-manuscript-migrations/oxford-tei-updates/blob/master/update_oxford_prov.rb in collaboration with the Mapping Manuscript Migrations project.
James Cummings Up-converted the markup using https://github.com/jamescummings/Bodleian-msDesc-ODD/blob/master/convertTolkien2Bodley.xsl
